3.description: Indiana 211 is a statewide or regional referral resource for Hoosiers affected by the criminal justice system. Free statewide information and referral service connecting Hoosiers to health and human services including housing, food, utilities, employment, and crisis support. Community navigators help callers find local programs by need and ZIP code. Indiana 211 is a referral line operated under FSSA—not a direct-service provider. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Indiana. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
3.description_es: Indiana 211 es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para hoosiers afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en en todo Indiana. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.

5.description: Indiana Legal Services — Statewide Intake serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Indiana. Indiana's largest nonprofit civil legal aid provider serving low-income residents in all 92 counties with housing, benefits, family law, consumer issues, and record-related civil matters. Centralized intake connects callers to the appropriate regional office. ILS does not handle criminal defense cases. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
5.description_es: Indiana Legal Services — Statewide Intake ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en en todo Indiana. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.

8.description: Recovery Works — Indiana DMHA is a statewide or regional referral resource for Hoosiers affected by the criminal justice system. Indiana Division of Mental Health and Addiction voucher program funding treatment and recovery housing for justice-involved adults without other payer sources. Criminal justice partners submit referrals through the online intake system; liaisons match participants to certified providers. Referral required—not a walk-in crisis line. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Indiana. 13.description: Indiana Bureau of Motor Vehicles (BMV) serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Indiana. State agency providing driver's licenses, state identification cards, vehicle registration, and reinstatement services. Reentry partners and community programs often assist returning citizens with documentation needed for employment and benefits. myBMV.com supports many transactions online. Fees, proof of identity, and residency documents apply; reentry programs and legal aid partners often help gather required paperwork. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.

17.description: Marion County Reentry Coalition is a regional reentry coalition serving Marion County. Partner agencies, faith communities, and workforce organizations meet regularly to share resources and warm referrals for people leaving jail or prison, on probation or parole, and their families. Coordinators help returning citizens connect to housing, employment, treatment, legal aid, and benefits through local partners. This is primarily a networking and navigation council—not an emergency shelter, cash assistance program, or crisis line.
17.description_es: Marion County Reentry Coalition es una coalición regional de reinserción que sirve condado de Marion. Agencias aliadas, comunidades de fe y organizaciones laborales se reúnen para compartir recursos y referencias directas para personas que salen de la cárcel o prisión, en libertad condicional o vigilada, y sus familias. Los coordinadores ayudan a conectar a ciudadanos que regresan con vivienda, empleo, tratamiento, asistencia legal y beneficios a través de aliados locales. Es principalmente un consejo de red y navegación—no un refugio de emergencia, programa de efectivo ni línea de crisis.
17.eligibility: Justice-involved residents of Marion County and family members seeking local reentry partner referrals; coalition does not determine partner agency eligibility.
17.eligibility_es: Residentes con antecedentes penales de condado de Marion y familiares que buscan referencias de aliados locales de reinserción; la coalición no determina la elegibilidad de agencias aliadas.

51.description: Marion County Community Corrections is a statewide or regional referral resource for Hoosiers affected by the criminal justice system. County-administered community corrections program in Marion County providing intermediate sanctions, work release, home detention, and reentry coordination for justice-involved individuals referred by courts or IDOC. Referral required through court or correctional system—not walk-in enrollment for most programs. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in Marion County. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
51.description_es: Marion County Community Corrections es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para hoosiers afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en condado de Marion. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.
51.notes: For current intake, call (317) 327-1111; visit https://www.in.gov/idoc/community-corrections/community-corrections-and-justice-reinvestment-grants/grant-entity-contacts/. Monday–Friday business hours; contact county office.
